_comment: Seed corpus of release groups / uploaders whose encodes are reliably bad. These names get scrawled across torrent filenames and are normally scrubbed out by movie_naming; here we keep them so a file can carry a 'who did this' verdict. Each entry is an editorial claim about a group's track record, not a measurement of any single local file — the badge it drives is presentation over the existing diagnosis, never a detector of its own. Tiers: 'punt' = punt on sight, every drop is the wettest fart of a file; 'warn' = hit-or-miss, inspect before trusting.

punt.label: Known moron
punt.note: This group signs its name to reliably bad encodes. Punt without thinking.

warn.label: Suspect encoder
warn.note: Hit-or-miss group. Some drops are fine, many are not — inspect before trusting it.

encoders.name: YIFY
encoders.note: The undisputed, reigning king of WTF encoding decisions. Tiny files dressed up as HD, crushed bitrates, 2.0 audio welded to everything — and it top-seeds every public tracker, so a big seed count means nothing. Signs every release; punt on sight.

encoders.name: MkvCage
encoders.note: Serial re-encoder that keeps rebranding to dodge its own reputation. Same crushed re-encodes under a fresh name each time.

encoders.name: moviesbyrizzo
encoders.note: Roughly 75% baffling, 25% surprisingly considered, as if the settings are picked by coin flip. Earns a softer warning than an outright punt because it covers genuinely niche titles no one else touches.

encoders.name: Ganool
encoders.note: Legacy mass re-encoder. Mostly small lossy rips, but occasionally the only copy of an otherwise-unavailable title.

encoders.name: PSA
encoders.note: Small x265 rips with a devoted following. Genuinely efficient when they land, mushy and over-compressed when they don't — inspect before trusting.
